title: Welcome to My Portfolio
text:
Hi, my name is Vianey Villegas, and I am a student interested in marketing managment, digital marketing, and creative strategy.
This portfolio shares a few projects I have worked on while learning how brands use data to understand customers, improve campaigns, and communicate more effectively.

title: Welcome to My Portfolio
text:
About Me
I am currently building my skills in marketing analytics, digital marketing, and data storytelling. I enjoy projects that combine creativity with startegy, like filming content for social media.

title: Welcome to My Portfolio
text:
Career Interest
My goal is to work in a digital marketing related role where I can help a company understand its audience, improve campaign performance, and make better decisions using data.

title: Google Analytics Portfolio
text:
In this project, I learned how to use Google Analytics to understand website traffic, user behavior, and performance data. I learned how analytics can help improve a website by showing where visitors come from, what pages they view, and how they interact with content.

What I Learned

How to track website visitors
How to analyze traffic sources
How to understand user behavior
How to read reports and key metrics
How data helps improve website performance



Skills I Gained

Data analysis
Website performance tracking
Understanding user engagement
Reading analytics reports
Making decisions using data

title: Marketing Projects
text:
For this project, I reviewed a sample social media campaign and looked at engagement, reach, and audience response.
Main question:
Which type of content performed best?
What I learned:
Posts with clear visuals and short captions usually had stronger engagement.

title: Marketing Projects
text:
Project 2: Customer Persona

I created a customer persona based on demographic and interest-based information.
Persona:
A college student who likes affordable fashion, simple beauty products, and brands that feel authentic online.
Marketing idea:
Use short videos, student discounts, and relatable content to connect with this audience.

title: Marketing Projects
text:
Project 3: Email Marketing Example

This project focused on creating a simple email campaign idea for a student-friendly brand.
Goal:
Increase clicks to a product page.
Metrics:
Open rate, click-through rate, and conversion rate.

title: Digital Marketing Projects
text: This page includes a few TikTok videos I created or selected as examples of digital marketing content. These videos show how short form content can be used to build engagement, share a message, and connect with an audience.

title: Digital Marketing Projects
text: This page includes a few TikTok videos I created or selected as examples of digital marketing content. These videos show how short form content can be used to build engagement, share a message, and connect with an audience.

title: Digital Marketing Projects
text:
TikTok Video 1
Campaign Goal:
Increase brand awareness through short-form video content.
Target Audience:
College students and young adults interested in lifestyle, beauty, fashion, or student-friendly products.
Marketing Strategy:
This video uses a quick hook, simple visuals, and a relatable message to keep viewers interested.
Watch TikTok Video 1

title: Digital Marketing Projects
text:
TikTok Video 2
Campaign Goal:
Increase brand awareness by promoting their new sunscreen through short form video content.
Target Audience:
People who are looking for an everyday use sunscreen.
Marketing Strategy:
Applying product and demonsatring the results right away.
Watch TikTok Video 2

title: Digital Marketing Projects
text:
TikTok Video 3
Campaign Goal:
Increase brand awareness of their new product line of sunglasses wipes through short form video content.
Target Audience:
College students who want to keep their sunglasses nice and clean at all times.
Marketing Strategy:
This video focuses on authenticity and a casual style, which can make the content feel more personal and less like an ad.
Watch TikTok Video 3

title: Digital Marketing Projects
text:
What I Learned
Short-form videos can be powerful in digital marketing because they are quick, visual, and easy to share. TikTok content works best when it feels natural, relatable, and clear within the first few seconds.

title: My Projects
text:
Marketing Analytics Case Study


Overview
In my Consumer Behavior course, I worked on a project analyzing the brand ALO Yoga and developed a creative brand extension into the pet market.
The project explored how ALO’s identity could expand into pet products while maintaining brand consistency.



What I Did

Analyzed ALO’s target audience and brand positioning

Applied consumer behavior theories

Developed a pet-focused product line concept

Created branding and marketing strategies




Key Insight
The extension shows how lifestyle brands can expand into new markets by aligning with their customers’ values and behaviors.



Presentation
View My Project Slides

title: My Projects
section: Creating a New App to Target College Students
text:
Creating a New App to Target College Students


Overview
In my computer information systems class, our final project was to create an app we think targets college students. My team and I created Spend Smarties, a budgeting app that will help students budget their income and save money. It tracks every transcations and gives budegts per category, and it connects directy to your bank account.



What I Did

researched related apps to create a perceptual map and see where the gap is in the market
I tested the app with 10 people and got thei opinion on the app.
created a survey and then analyzed the data




Key Insight
Many students struggle to control their budgeting because they don’t have the tools to track, so our Spend Smarties offers those tools to facilitate budgeting.



Presentation
View My Project Slides

title: Shiny Live
text: Shinylive is helpful when you need to use Shiny apps. it avoids needing a server because Shinylive removes it by simply running the information directly from the device. To use Shinylive, you need R codes. In other words it’s more simply than Shiny Apps. Shinylive should be used when you want to teach someone about interactive websites, want easier deployment, and if you want a simple interactive dashboard.

title: Interactive Dashboard
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)

── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.6
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ purrr     1.2.1     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors

library(gt)


df <- read.csv("Employment - National - Weekly.csv")

# Create date column
df$date <- as.Date(paste(df$year, df$month, df$day_endofweek, sep = "-"))


ggplot(df, aes(x = date, y = emp)) +
  geom_line(color = "pink") +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Employment")









df <- read.csv("Employment - National - Weekly.csv")

df$date <- as.Date(paste(df$year, df$month, df$day_endofweek, sep = "-"))

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    emp_incq1 = as.numeric(emp_incq1),
    emp_incq2 = as.numeric(emp_incq2),
    emp_incq3 = as.numeric(emp_incq3),
    emp_incq4 = as.numeric(emp_incq4)
  )

Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
ℹ In argument: `emp_incq4 = as.numeric(emp_incq4)`.
Caused by warning:
! NAs introduced by coercion

df_long <- df %>%
  select(date, emp_incq1, emp_incq2, emp_incq3, emp_incq4) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("emp_incq"),
    names_to = "income_group",
    values_to = "employment"
  )

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = income_group, y = employment, fill = income_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Employment by Income Group",
    x = "Income Group",
    y = "Employment",
    fill = "Income Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Warning: Removed 174 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_col()`).









::: callout note emp_incq# stands for employment, income quartile, and # is the group :::

library(tidyverse)

df <- read.csv("Employment - National - Weekly.csv")

df$date <- as.Date(paste(df$year, df$month, df$day_endofweek, sep = "-"))


ggplot(df, aes(x = emp)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "pink") +
  labs(x = "Employment Change", y = "Count")

title: Shiny App Summary
text:
When you think of the Shiny app, think of a website that is interactive, that was built in Rstudio. The Shiny app creates interactive dashboards. It makes it more fun because instead of having a static website, the viewers are able to interactive with the data visuals like:

click buttons
choose different options
move sliders

To create the interactive dashbaord, you have to have a user interface which is what the users see and a server.
User Interface - the intercative dashboards - titles - charts/graphs
Server - codes - controls dashbaord - process information - updates the graphs

title: Reveal js Presentation
text:
This presentation was created using Reveal.js through Quarto to showcase my work in a clear, interactive slide format. It demonstrates my ability to organize and present technical concepts effectively while using modern web-based tools. By including this presentation, I highlight my understanding of advanced R programming and my ability to communicate data-driven insights in a professional and visually engaging way.
